{{Short description="Spatial period of a periodic wave}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Wavelength
| image = Sine wavelength.svg
| caption = Wavelength <math>\lambda</math> of a sinusoidal wave: the distance over which the wave's shape repeats.
| topics = [[Wave]], [[Frequency]], [[Electromagnetic wave]], [[Electromagnetic spectrum]]
| symbol = λ
| SI_unit = [[metre]] (m)
}}

In [[physics]], the '''wavelength''' is the spatial period of a periodic [[wave]] — the distance over which the wave's shape repeats. It is the distance between consecutive corresponding points of the same phase, such as crests, troughs, or [[zero crossing]]s.

For a sinusoidal wave:
:<math>y(x,t) = A\sin(kx - \omega t)</math>

where <math>k = 2\pi/\lambda</math> is the [[wavenumber]] and <math>\lambda</math> is the wavelength.

== Relationship to frequency ==
Wavelength <math>\lambda</math> is related to [[frequency]] <math>f</math> (or <math>\nu</math>) and wave speed <math>v</math> by:

:<math>v = \lambda f = \frac{\lambda}{T}</math>

where <math>T = 1/f</math> is the [[period (physics)|period]].

== Electromagnetic waves ==
{{Main|Electromagnetic wave}}
For [[electromagnetic wave]]s in vacuum:
:<math>c = \lambda\nu</math>

The [[visible spectrum]] ranges from ~380 nm (violet) to ~750 nm (red).

== De Broglie wavelength ==
{{Main|De Broglie wavelength}}
In [[quantum mechanics]], all particles have an associated wavelength:
:<math>\lambda = \frac{h}{p}</math>
where <math>h</math> is the [[Planck constant]] and <math>p</math> is [[momentum]].

== Matter waves ==
* [[Sound wave]]s in air: <math>\lambda = v_s / f</math>, where <math>v_s \approx 343</math> m/s
* Waves on a string: <math>\lambda = 2L/n</math> for standing waves (<math>n = 1, 2, 3, \ldots</math>)

== Angular wavenumber ==
The [[wavenumber]] <math>k</math> is related to wavelength by:
:<math>k = \frac{2\pi}{\lambda}</math>

This is commonly used in wave equations and [[quantum mechanics]].

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[De Broglie wavelength]]
* [[Electromagnetic spectrum]]
* [[Frequency]]
* [[Wave]]
* [[Wavenumber]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Wavelength| ]]
[[Category:Wave mechanics]]
[[Category:Physical quantities]]
